New: In-app version history viewer (Tools → 📋 Version history): a dropdown to pick any release, showing that version's New/Updated/Fixed/Removed bullets right in the browser — no need to leave the app to read CHANGELOG.md.

data/changelog.json + scripts/build_changelog_json.py — CHANGELOG.md stays the single source of truth (the normal, human-editable format), and this script parses it into the JSON the browser actually reads. Same one-way build-step pattern as the search index: edit the markdown, run the script, ship the JSON.
Updated: Fixed several markdown line-wraps in CHANGELOG.md that broke mid compound-word or mid slash-chain (e.g. a line ending in "multi-" with "cloud" starting the next line) — harmless when GitHub renders the markdown (it collapses soft line breaks), but produced a stray extra space once parsed into plain bullet text for the in-app viewer.

New: Cloud Platforms section (under Fundamentals, alongside OSI and DNS): IaaS/PaaS/SaaS service models with real examples and a comparison table; deployment models (public/private/hybrid/multi-cloud) with the actual trade-offs between them; a cloud networking page tying cloud concepts back to on-prem networking specifically (VPC/VNet, site-to-site VPN vs. Direct Connect/ExpressRoute, DNS across environments, addressing-overlap pitfalls) rather than generic cloud-computing trivia.
Shared Responsibility Model tool — click On-Premises/IaaS/PaaS/SaaS, see exactly which of 9 layers (data down to the physical facility) you manage vs. the provider, for that model.
6 new flashcards (Cloud category, 80 total), with a matching filter button.
Updated: Search index rebuilt to include the 4 new Cloud panels (78 → now indexed).
Stale flashcard-count references on the Home page updated to 80.

New: Global quick-search — press / anywhere (outside a text field) to open it. Matches page titles, headings, reference-table entries, dropdown options, and form labels.
Multi-word AND matching in search ("SOA record" finds the SOA panel even though that phrase never appears verbatim).
Search result ranking that weights real headings above incidental table/label mentions.
Permanent search-limitations disclaimer in the search overlay footer.
data/search-index.json + scripts/build_search_index.py — the index is a static file, regenerated by the script after any content edit; not built automatically on page load.
Version badge in the header, plus this changelog format itself and TOOL_VERSIONS.md for per-tool version tracking.
Updated: Top nav: 12 items → 8. DNS merged into Fundamentals, Switching into Routing, NAT into ACL, Wireless into QoS — same "merge sidebars into one umbrella" pattern already used for Security. Each merged topic keeps its own home card and its own sidebar group.
Header split into two rows: branding + search on row one, nav gets a full-width row to itself — fixes the recurring nav-overflow problem properly instead of continuing to shrink nav-link text.
Sidebar no longer shows on the Home page — reclaims ~210px of width for the home-card grid. Still shows normally, correctly aligned to the active umbrella section, on every other page.
Removed: The sb-home sidebar shortcut list — fully redundant with the home cards and global search.
Standalone DNS / Switching / NAT / Wireless top-nav entries (content preserved, just relocated under their merged umbrella).

New: Fundamentals section: the OSI model — all 7 layers with a visual stack diagram, encapsulation/de-encapsulation walkthrough with a worked example, layer-by-layer deep dive, OSI vs TCP/IP comparison, troubleshooting mapped to each layer.
DNS section: how resolution works (recursive vs iterative, with a diagram), every major record type with real examples, and the interactive DNS record builder tool.
Security section additions: NGFW (App-ID/DPI/IPS/TLS inspection) and Cisco ISE (802.1X/MAB, posture, TrustSec/SGT) reference content. No configuration tools for these two, by design.
Design Helper: perimeter firewall, DMZ zone, and Wireless LAN Controller as optional appliances, with a strict bordered External/DMZ/Internal visual split and matching device-click modals.
18 new flashcards (OSI ×6, DNS ×6, Security ×6) — 74 total.
CHANGELOG.md and an in-app "What's new" card on the Home page.
Updated: Design Helper: layer-toggle legend moved to a vertical side column; requirements form and detail cards became collapsible.
Top nav: VPN merged into Security (IPsec is a security topic either way) after the section count started genuinely overflowing the nav at 1280px — confirmed by measurement, not assumption.
Home page hero pills and card tags updated to match the new sections and flashcard count (several were stale at "56 cards").
Fixed: Design Helper: the Internal zone's bordered background started 3px below the first device row's actual top edge, so that row visually poked through the border. Now has clean 12px clearance.
Design Helper: a device-icon glyph referenced currentColor with no color context set, silently rendering the wrong color.

New: Cisco banner (motd/login/exec) generator, with live delimiter-collision detection — warns and auto-swaps the delimiter if it appears inside the message text instead of silently generating a broken banner.
Collapsible "How to use this tool" hint on every tool panel.
Collapsible sidebar, state persisted via localStorage.
Updated: ACL Builder and Config Validator moved fully into the Tools section (previously lived in both Tools and the ACL section's own sidebar).
Flashcards moved off the top nav into Tools, alongside Quick Reference.
Top nav: Tools moved to the end of the list.
Fixed: Flashcards: grading buttons used to look clickable even before a card was flipped, but did nothing when clicked in that state — a dead click that read as a broken tool. (Superseded by the v1.2.0 flashcard redesign in the next release — see TOOL_VERSIONS.md.)
Flashcards: if data/flashcards.json fails to load, the card area now explains why instead of sitting on "Loading…" forever.

New: Interactive Design Helper: suggested topology, device/port counts, a VLAN plan, and a starter IOS config generated from site requirements.
Visual SVG topology diagram for the Design Helper — toggleable layers, clickable device icons opening a device-config viewer.
STP deep-dive panels (flavors/PVST+/RSTP/MST, port states and roles, protection features, root bridge design) and a full Network Design reference section.
Inline SVG diagrams for ACL in/out direction and NAT inside/outside local/global addressing.
Save/load designs via a PHP + SQLite backend.
Updated: Architecture: split the single 300KB index.html into index.html + css/style.css + focused JS modules + JSON data files + the PHP API — enables permanent Docker hosting instead of only offline single-file use.
113 official reference links (Cisco/IETF/IEEE/NIST) added across existing panels.
Fixed: OSPF cost calculator: a duplicate id="ospf-cost" meant getElementById returned the wrong element; renamed the output span and fixed the divide-by-zero case.
Flashcards/Quick Reference not displaying: the page-navigation function only ever activated the content panel for the home page. Generalized to any page whose id matches a panel.

New: Initial release: a single self-contained offline HTML file covering ACL, NAT, routing protocols (OSPF/EIGRP/BGP), switching (VLANs/STP/EtherChannel), IPsec VPN, QoS, and Wireless.
Interactive subnet/wildcard/OSPF-cost calculators, an ACL builder, a VLAN planner, and a 56-card flashcard deck.
